summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 6 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:12` — Custom YAML parser handles only the current flat schema; silent failures on future schema evolution (multiline strings, nested maps). Add a migration-path comment for future maintainers.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:221` — Keyword substring matching ("api" matches "capital") is documented in code but not in user-facing runbook authoring docs. Will cause confusion for custom runbook authors.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:438` — Forced-runbook log says `forced: {name}` but doesn't indicate it was triggered by `--runbook`. Consider `(via --runbook)` for clarity.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:221` — Keyword substring matching ("api" matches "capital") is documented in code but not in user-facing docs. Users authoring custom runbooks will hit surprising false positives. Backlog item for documentation.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:446` — `resolveRunbookTasks` can return `[]` if a matched runbook's tasks are all `include:` references to nonexistent IDs. This would produce an empty tasks array downstream. Add a guard: if resolved is empty, warn and fall through.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — `isSafeRegex()` heuristic bypassed by alternation-based ReDoS patterns like `(a|a)+`. Low practical impact (short inputs, repo-controlled YAML). **Backlog item.**
